tools.name: list_tracked_apis
tools.description: List all tracked APIs with metadata and latest changelog status. Filter by category (ai, payments, developer-tools, communications, infrastructure, email).

tools.name: fetch_changelog
tools.description: Fetch versioned changelog entries for a specific API (openai, anthropic, stripe, github, twilio, cloudflare, sendgrid, slack). Filter by date and breaking-only.

tools.name: diff_versions
tools.description: Compare changelog state between two dates for an API. Shows what was added or removed in the range.

tools.name: detect_breaking_changes
tools.description: Detect breaking changes across one or multiple APIs. Filter by severity (critical, high, medium, low) and date. Omit api_id to scan all APIs.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Full-text search across all changelog entries. Searches title, summary, and change descriptions.

tools.name: subscribe_feed
tools.description: Register a webhook or email to receive changelog update notifications. Returns a subscription ID.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_tracked_apis, subscribe_feed · Public sink: list_tracked_apis, subscribe_feed.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_tracked_apis, subscribe_feed · Public sink: list_tracked_apis, subscribe_feed

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
